title: TrainingService._queues created unbounded — no maxsize
notes: Set asyncio.Queue(maxsize=1024) and handle QueueFull on producer side — drop stale metrics when consumer is slow/disconnected

title: _injection_queue unbounded — no maxsize
notes: Set asyncio.Queue(maxsize=128) and catch QueueFull on put_nowait call

title: asyncio.run() inside sync methods called via run_in_executor — latent fragility
notes: Document that _load_docs MUST be called from thread pool worker, not event loop; or refactor to make async

title: asyncio.run() in demo model provider — ThreadPoolExecutor workaround for running loop
notes: Document running-loop workaround; consider making _load_demo_docs async to eliminate workaround

title: MPSSamplerThread not stopped on normal completion — only stopped in exception handler
notes: Add mps_thread.stop() in finally: block at end of _run_training() or attach to _cleanup callback at line 587

title: No mid-step cancellation — threading.Event checked only at step boundaries
notes: Document as known limitation; consider SIGINT handler for mid-step cancellation — steps are fast so risk is low

title: No task cancellation or queue drainage on FastAPI lifespan shutdown
notes: Cancel in-flight _tasks and call supervisor.stop_all() in lifespan shutdown; signal/drain training queues

title: TrainingService._queues dict — shared state accessed from multiple threads
notes: Dict only mutated on event loop thread; progress_callback captures queue ref at closure creation time — add doc comment

title: _demo_provider._model/_chars — warmup thread writes without _DEMO_TRAIN_LOCK
notes: Warmup thread writes _demo_provider._model and _chars at lines 264-265 without holding lock — races with get_model() first check at line 307

title: ThreadPoolExecutor workaround for running event loop — creates new pool per invocation
notes: Move ThreadPoolExecutor to module-level singleton to avoid repeated creation; document pattern

title: Demo model warmup daemon thread — fire-and-forget, no stop mechanism
notes: Daemon thread doesn't prevent exit; try/except inside mitigates unhandled exceptions. Consider join with timeout on shutdown.

title: _DEMO_TRAIN_LOCK with double-checked locking — training held under lock for 30-60s
notes: Move training outside lock; use asyncio.Lock for request-level concurrency instead of threading.Lock

title: _PID_DIR and _processes — module-level mutable state
notes: Rename _PID_DIR to PID_DIR (constant); document single-thread access pattern for _processes dict

title: MPSSamplerThread — dedicated daemon thread with own event loop
notes: Reference implementation for thread+loop pattern. Daemon thread with new_event_loop() and finally: loop.close().

title: MPSSamplerThread creates own event loop — correct pattern
notes: Correct pattern: new_event_loop() + run_until_complete() + finally: close(). No cross-loop confusion.

title: asyncio.get_event_loop() used 24+ times — should migrate to get_running_loop()
notes: Pervasive style issue in Python 3.10+. get_running_loop() preferred. All call sites are in async functions on main event loop — functionally correct.

title: MLflowService.process — single-thread access to subprocess reference
notes: Only accessed from lifespan startup/shutdown (sequential). No concurrent access. INFO-level documentation finding.

title: asyncio.create_task for training run — has cleanup callback, no shutdown cancellation
notes: Task has add_done_callback(_cleanup) but is not explicitly cancelled on FastAPI shutdown.

title: _bootstrap_lock TOCTOU race in rebootstrap_demo — check before acquire allows concurrent requests
notes: Remove the `if not _bootstrap_lock.locked()` check; use `async with _bootstrap_lock:` directly. Bootstrap is idempotent so data corruption risk is low, but 409 response is unreliable.

title: asyncio.get_event_loop() from sync callback in worker thread — deprecated and may fail
notes: Replace get_event_loop()+create_task() with run_coroutine_threadsafe(). try/except Exception swallows errors silently — CLI training path only.

title: new_event_loop() without set_event_loop() — intentional isolation
notes: Loop is used via direct reference (self._loop.run_until_complete) — no code calls get_event_loop() from this thread. Add comment explaining isolation.
